economic_notes: Deninu K'ue First Nation is economically dependent on government funding from Indigenous and Northern Affairs Canada and the Government of the Northwest Territories. Subsidiary Deninu K'ue Holdings Limited was not consolidated due to incomplete financial records.
notes: Audit is qualified on three grounds: (1) subsidiary Deninu K'ue Holdings Limited not consolidated due to incomplete financial records; (2) management unable to determine source of material adjustment to miscellaneous revenue; (3) significant cash transactions to members not susceptible to satisfactory audit verification. Financial statements are non-consolidated. Fiscal year 2020 statements were signed off February 9, 2022 — a significant reporting delay. The IBA Fund surplus is transferred to the General Operating Fund each year. Revenue and expense totals combine General Fund and IBA Fund. Accumulated surplus calculated as sum of all fund balances: General $1,858,157 + Capital $14,207 + Investment $10 = $1,872,374.
